summary: Completed comprehensive testing of the enrollment verification exit code fix. All static checks, runtime tests, artifact validation, and backend endpoint tests passed successfully.
passed_tests: Static: os_info.py exists with only stdlib imports (from __future__ and import platform)
Static: enroll.py imports from .os_info and does NOT import from .collectors
Static: __main__.py _cmd_bootstrap has 3 'return 5' statements for proper error handling
Static: install_helpers.cmd has diagnostic messages for exit codes 4, 5, and 1
Static: MSI staging files match source files
Runtime: bootstrap with missing seed directory returns exit code 4 (not 1)
Runtime: bootstrap with invalid token returns exit code 5 (not 1) with backend HTTP error in stderr
Runtime: bootstrap with valid enrollment code returns exit code 0, token redacted to empty string, provisioned=true
Runtime: importing digital_twin_agent.enroll does NOT import collectors package (verified via sys.modules)
Artifact: MSI is valid OLE2 format (D0CF11E0A1B11AE1 signature, 20MB)
Artifact: EXE is valid Windows PE format (MZ signature, 19MB)
Artifact: MSI contains agent/digital_twin_agent/os_info.py with correct stdlib-only imports
Artifact: MSI's enroll.py contains 'from .os_info import short_os_info' and no collectors imports
Backend: POST /api/auth/login with admin credentials returns 200 with access_token
Backend: POST /api/enrollment/codes with bearer token returns 200 with enrollment code
Backend: POST /api/enrollment/enroll with valid code returns 200 with device_id, device_api_key, org_id
